[SURVEY] Overall satisfaction with current product: 'Somewhat satisfied'
[SURVEY] Uses 3 brands, suggesting willingness to try within parameters
nodes.predictive_value: When considering a switch, this person would likely need clear, comparative evidence showing superiority over their current satisfactory options.

nodes.label: expects-brand-to-extend-core-competencies
nodes.description: Evaluates whether new concepts fit Dove's established expertise (gentle cleansing, moisturization) while accepting moderate stretching into adjacent areas (technology, personalization).
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] (For all 5 Dove concepts) 'Fits somewhat well with what you expect from Dove'
[SURVEY] ...uses proven micellar technology from Dove's face care range...
[SURVEY] ...the promise of a fast, 60-second cleansing that uses proven micellar technology from Dove's face care range...
nodes.predictive_value: When Dove launches a radically different product (e.g., exfoliating scrub), this person would likely question the brand fit more than for tech-enhanced gentle cleansers.

nodes.label: clinical-backing-and-ingredient-transparency-seeker
nodes.description: Consistently demands to see detailed clinical evidence, ingredient lists, and data behind product claims before believing them, especially for skin-related benefits.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] I would like body washes to improve their ingredient transparency and clinical backing, especially for oily, acne-prone skin.
[SURVEY] I would want clearer ingredient details and clinical evidence supporting the 60-second claim...
[SURVEY] I would want more clinical data on the lipid-lock technology and ingredient specifics...
[SURVEY] I would want clearer evidence of the clinical studies mentioned and ingredient concentration details...
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept with a skin-benefit claim, this person would likely search for or request access to clinical studies and a full ingredient breakdown before considering a purchase.

nodes.label: risk-averse-for-oily-acne-prone-skin
nodes.description: Prioritizes skin safety and expresses consistent concern that new products could irritate their specific skin type, seeking reassurance before trial.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Could irritate my skin (selected for 5/5 concepts)
[SURVEY] ...plus assurance that it suits oily, acne-prone skin without causing irritation.
[SURVEY] ...plus confirmation it won’t irritate my skin type.
[SURVEY] ...clear assurances that the product won’t cause irritation for oily, acne-prone skin are necessary.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product concept, this person would likely first evaluate if it is explicitly formulated for or proven safe on oily, acne-prone skin, and dismiss options that lack this assurance.

nodes.label: pragmatic-time-and-routine-efficiency-seeker
nodes.description: Attracted to innovations that demonstrably save time (60-second wash) or simplify routines (skip lotion step), rating these benefits highly in appeal.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] The most appealing part is the promise of getting completely clean in just 60 seconds... which sounds efficient for busy days.
[SURVEY] The idea of moisturizing skin straight from the shower for 12 hours without lotion is appealing as it simplifies the routine...
[SURVEY] Ranked 'Dove Skip' and 'Dove 60-Second Body Spray' as their top 2 most appealing concepts.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept that promises time savings or routine simplification, this person would likely show elevated interest but will scrutinize the plausibility of the time/step-saving claim.

nodes.label: innovation-appreciator-but-not-early-adopter
nodes.description: Finds innovative features appealing and rates them as unique, but holds back from immediate purchase due to skepticism and a 'wait-and-see' attitude towards unproven claims.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Rated 'Dove Skin ID' highest on the 'Old-fashioned-Innovative' scale (5).
[SURVEY] Don't trust new products (selected as a concern for Dove Skin ID).
[SURVEY] For all five innovative Dove concepts, purchase intent was 'Might or might not buy' or lower, despite finding them 'somewhat unique' and 'somewhat relevant'.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a highly innovative product concept, this person would likely express interest and intellectual curiosity but would delay purchase until seeing user reviews, expert endorsements, or more proof of efficacy.

nodes.label: price-anchor-around-350-rupees
nodes.description: Has a specific, moderate price expectation for premium body wash products, considering ₹350 acceptable while viewing prices of ₹699-₹799 as 'steep'.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] What would you expect to pay for your most preferred concept? (Enter amount in rupees) A: 350
[SURVEY] The price at ₹799 seems a bit high...
[SURVEY] The price point of ₹699 for the first order is steep...
[SURVEY] 'Price might be too high' was a selected concern for all five concepts.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept priced significantly above ₹350, this person would likely hesitate and require exceptional justification (e.g., proven superior results, large size) to consider it.

nodes.label: brand-experienced-but-not-loyal
nodes.description: Uses multiple mainstream brands (Dove, Pears, Lux, Fiama) concurrently, is only 'somewhat satisfied' with current products, and is open to switching for a better solution.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Which body wash or shower gel brands have you used in the past 3 months? A: ['Dove', 'Pears', 'Lux', 'Fiama', 'Other brand']
[SURVEY] Overall, how satisfied are you with the body wash or shower gel you currently use most often? A: Somewhat satisfied
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product concept from an established or new brand, this person would likely evaluate it on its standalone merits rather than defaulting to their current brand, provided it meets their core criteria.

nodes.label: claims-skeptic-requires-proof
nodes.description: Default rating for believability of new product claims is 'somewhat believable,' indicating inherent skepticism that requires evidence to overcome.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] How believable are the claims made about the [Dove product]? A: Somewhat believable (answered identically for all 5 concepts)
[SURVEY] 'Might not work as claimed' was a selected concern for all five concepts.
[SURVEY] I would want clearer ingredient details and clinical evidence supporting the 60-second claim...
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an advertisement with a bold claim, this person would likely be unmoved by aspirational language alone and would respond better to ads featuring data, dermatologist quotes, or 'proof' demonstrations.

nodes.label: convenient-availability-checker
nodes.description: Factors product availability at their usual shopping channels (like Nykaa or Amazon) into their purchase consideration, noting unavailability as a specific concern.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] 'Might not be available where I shop' (selected as a concern for two concepts)
[SURVEY] Availability in usual stores like Nykaa or Amazon would also help.
nodes.predictive_value: When interested in a new product, this person would likely first check their preferred online retailers before actively seeking it out in physical stores or specialty channels.

nodes.label: data-driven-personalization-trustor
nodes.description: Expresses higher trust in personalization that is based on data (like AI skin analysis) versus more generic or fragrance-based customization.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] The AI skin analysis feature... promises a tailored solution for my oily, acne-prone skin. The combination of photo analysis and personal responses suggests a data-driven approach that I trust more than generic products.
[SURVEY] Rated 'Dove Skin ID' highest on 'Not for me-Made for me' scale (4) compared to other concepts.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a personalized skincare concept, this person would likely be more receptive to one that uses diagnostic technology or questionnaires over one based solely on fragrance preference.

nodes.label: cleansing-effectiveness-and-value-maximizer
nodes.description: Rates 'Cleansing effectiveness' and 'Value for money' as the most important factors (5/5) when choosing a body wash, above fragrance, brand trust, or convenience.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] How important are each of these factors when choosing a body wash? A: {'Cleansing effectiveness': 5, 'Value for money': 5, ...}
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a purchase, this person would likely conduct a mental cost-benefit analysis focusing primarily on how well the product cleanses and whether its price is justified by its performance.

nodes.label: daily-body-wash-user
nodes.description: Uses body wash on a daily basis, indicating regular consumption and frequent purchase cycles.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: How often do you personally use body wash or shower gel? A: Daily
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would likely evaluate its suitability for daily use and consider purchase frequency.

nodes.label: moderately-satisfied-with-current-product
nodes.description: Is only somewhat satisfied with current body wash, indicating openness to switch if a better option appears.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: Overall, how satisfied are you with the body wash or shower gel you currently use most often? A: Somewhat satisfied
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product that addresses their specific needs, they would be more likely to switch because they are not fully committed to their current product.

nodes.label: multi-brand-user
nodes.description: Has used multiple brands (Dove, Pears, Lux, other) in the past 3 months, showing no strong brand loyalty.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: Which body wash or shower gel brands have you used in the past 3 months? Select all that apply. A: ["Dove", "Pears", "Lux", "Other brand"]
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product from any brand, they are likely to consider it based on attributes rather than brand allegiance.

nodes.label: high-value-for-money-importance
nodes.description: Rates 'Value for money' as 5 out of 5 in importance when choosing a body wash.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: How important are each of these factors when choosing a body wash? A: {"Value for money": 5}
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product concept with a high price, they would likely hesitate unless convinced of superior value relative to cost.

nodes.label: cleansing-effectiveness-maximizer
nodes.description: Rates 'Cleansing effectiveness' as 5 out of 5, the highest importance, and wants improved efficiency without extra scrubbing.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: How important are each of these factors when choosing a body wash? A: {"Cleansing effectiveness": 5}

[SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you most like to see improved in body wash or shower gel products? A: I would like body washes to improve their cleansing efficiency without needing extra scrubbing
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product that emphasizes deep cleansing or efficiency, they would be more attracted and likely to consider it over less effective options.

nodes.label: proof-seeker-for-claims
nodes.description: Consistently wants clinical proof, dermatologist endorsements, or evidence for product claims before believing them.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove 60-Second Body Spray concept? A: I would want clearer evidence or clinical proof that the 60-second formula truly cleans as claimed
[SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove Skip concept? A: I would want more clinical data or dermatologist endorsements to back the lipid-lock technology
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad with strong clinical evidence or dermatologist recommendation, they would be more likely to believe and consider the product.

nodes.label: risk-averse-to-new-products
nodes.description: Expresses concerns about new products not working as claimed, potential skin irritation, and being happy with current product.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What concerns, if any, would you have about trying the Dove 60-Second Body Spray? Select all that apply. A: ["Price might be too high", "Might not work as claimed", "Happy with current product"]
[SURVEY] Q: What concerns, if any, would you have about trying the Dove Skin ID? Select all that apply. A: ["Price might be too high", "Might not work as claimed", "Could irritate my skin"]
nodes.predictive_value: When introduced to a new product, they would likely need reassurance on efficacy and safety before trying, and may delay purchase until more evidence is available.

nodes.label: time-saving-appreciator
nodes.description: Values convenience and time-saving features, as seen in positive reactions to 60-second body spray and Skip (which saves time by eliminating lotion).
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, did you find most appealing about the Dove 60-Second Body Spray concept? A: The promise of getting completely clean in just 60 seconds... fits my need for quick, effective cleansing on busy days
[SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, did you find most appealing about the Dove Skip concept? A: ...eliminating the need for a separate lotion, which saves time and effort
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product that promises to save time or simplify routine, they would be more interested and likely to perceive higher value.

nodes.label: innovation-interested-but-cautious
nodes.description: Rates innovation as important (4 out of 5) but needs proof and reasonable price; shows interest in innovative concepts but with reservations.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: How important are each of these factors when choosing a body wash? A: {"Innovation / uniqueness": 4}
[SURVEY] Q: How would you describe the Dove Skin ID? Rate each pair of characteristics. A: {"Old-fashioned-Innovative": 5}
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a highly innovative product, they would be curious but would need strong evidence and value proposition to adopt.

nodes.label: scent-longevity-seeker
nodes.description: Expresses desire for longer-lasting fresh scent without being overpowering.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you most like to see improved in body wash or shower gel products? A: ...have longer-lasting fresh scent without being overpowering
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product that emphasizes long-lasting fresh scent, they would be more attracted and likely to consider it over products with weak or overpowering scents.

nodes.label: routine-fit-evaluator
nodes.description: Considers how well a product concept fits into current routine, rating fit as moderate (3) for multiple concepts.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: How well does the Dove 60-Second Body Spray concept fit with your current routine? A: 3
[SURVEY] Q: How well does the Dove Skip concept fit with your current routine? A: 3
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a new product, they would assess how easily it can be integrated into their existing shower routine, and may reject products that require significant changes.

nodes.label: moderate-brand-trust
nodes.description: Rates 'Brand trust and reputation' as 3 out of 5 in importance, indicating it matters but is not top priority.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: How important are each of these factors when choosing a body wash? A: {"Brand trust and reputation": 3}
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product from a trusted brand like Dove, they might give it some benefit of the doubt, but not enough to override concerns about price or effectiveness.

nodes.label: price-point-anchor-around-299
nodes.description: Expects to pay around 299 rupees for their most preferred concept (Dove Skip), setting a mental anchor for value.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What would you expect to pay for your most preferred concept? (Enter amount in rupees) A: 299
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product priced significantly above 299 rupees, they would likely perceive it as overpriced unless justified by exceptional benefits.

nodes.label: preference-for-tangible-benefits
nodes.description: Ranks concepts with tangible functional benefits (moisturization, time-saving) higher than emotional or social benefits (stress relief, fragrance bonding).
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: Now that you've seen all 5 concepts, please rank them from most to least appealing. Pick your top 3. A: ["Dove Skip", "Dove 60-Second Body Spray", "Dove Night Wash", "Dove Skin ID", "Dove Yours & Mine"]
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product that emphasizes functional benefits like time-saving or moisturization, they would be more likely to prefer it over one emphasizing emotional or social benefits.

nodes.label: clinical-data-responder
nodes.description: Specifically mentions clinical proof, clinical data, or clinical benefits in multiple concept improvement comments.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove 60-Second Body Spray concept? A: I would want clearer evidence or clinical proof
[SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove Night Wash concept? A: providing more evidence on the effectiveness of the calming benefits
nodes.predictive_value: When shown advertising that includes clinical study results, they would be more likely to believe and consider the product.

nodes.label: dermatologist-endorsement-seeker
nodes.description: Explicitly wants dermatologist endorsements to back technology and assure skin compatibility.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove Skip concept? A: I would want more clinical data or dermatologist endorsements to back the lipid-lock technology
nodes.predictive_value: When a product is recommended or endorsed by dermatologists, they would be more inclined to trust it and consider purchasing.

nodes.label: trial-size-preferrer
nodes.description: Wants smaller, more affordable trial sizes to test before committing to full-size, especially for higher-priced products.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove Yours & Mine concept? A: possibly a smaller, more affordable trial size to test before committing
nodes.predictive_value: When offered a trial size or sample at a lower cost, they would be more likely to try a new product, reducing perceived risk.

nodes.label: conditional-premium-payer
nodes.description: Willing to pay a premium price only if the product has proven ingredients or technology, and offers clear value.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What concerns, if any, would you have about trying the Dove Skip? Select all that apply. A: ["Price might be too high", "Might not work as claimed"]
[SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove Skip concept? A: before paying the premium price
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a premium-priced product, they would need strong evidence of superior performance to justify the cost, otherwise they will opt for cheaper alternatives.

nodes.label: multi-attribute-balancer
nodes.description: Considers multiple factors (cleansing, value, moisturization, convenience, etc.) when choosing, with no single factor overwhelmingly dominating except cleansing and value.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: How important are each of these factors when choosing a body wash? A: {"Cleansing effectiveness": 5, "Moisturization / skin hydration": 4, "Fragrance / scent quality": 3, "Gentleness on skin": 4, "Value for money": 5, "Innovation / uniqueness": 4, "Brand trust and reputation": 3, "Convenience / ease of use": 4}
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a product, they would weigh a combination of attributes, and a product that scores well across multiple factors would be favored over one excelling in only one area.

nodes.label: cautious-adopter
nodes.description: Purchase intent is mostly 'might or might not buy' for new concepts, indicating hesitation unless strongly convinced.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: Based on what you've seen, how likely would you be to buy the Dove 60-Second Body Spray? A: Might or might not buy
[SURVEY] Q: Based on what you've seen, how likely would you be to buy the Dove Night Wash? A: Might or might not buy
nodes.predictive_value: When introduced to a new product, they are unlikely to be an early adopter; they would wait for proof or reviews before purchasing.

nodes.label: cleansing-efficiency-focus
nodes.description: Wants improved cleansing efficiency without needing extra scrubbing, emphasizing effortless effectiveness.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you most like to see improved in body wash or shower gel products? A: improve their cleansing efficiency without needing extra scrubbing
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product that promises efficient cleansing with minimal effort, they would find it appealing and likely prioritize it over products requiring more work.

nodes.label: moisturization-integration-seeker
nodes.description: Likes the idea of a body wash that provides long-lasting moisturization, eliminating the need for separate lotion.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, did you find most appealing about the Dove Skip concept? A: 12-hour moisturization straight from the shower, eliminating the need for a separate lotion
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a 2-in-1 product that combines cleansing and moisturization, they would be interested as it simplifies their routine and saves time.

nodes.label: personalization-curious-but-skeptical
nodes.description: Interested in AI-based skin customization but skeptical about its effectiveness and price, requiring clinical backing and transparency.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, did you find most appealing about the Dove Skin ID concept? A: AI skin analysis technology that customizes the body wash for my unique skin needs
[SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove Skin ID concept? A: clearer clinical backing and ingredient transparency to ensure the AI customization truly works
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a personalized product, they would be intrigued but would need convincing evidence and reasonable price to try, otherwise they will stick with generic options.

nodes.label: fragrance-personalization-hesitant
nodes.description: Finds personalized fragrance appealing but hesitant due to high price and need for proof of lasting scent and skin compatibility.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, did you find most appealing about the Dove Yours & Mine concept? A: The personalized fragrance quiz... but the high price makes me hesitant without strong proof of lasting scent and skin compatibility
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a fragrance personalization concept, they would be interested only if priced reasonably and with evidence of performance, otherwise they will dismiss it as not worth the risk.

nodes.label: stress-relief-interest-moderate
nodes.description: Likes the idea of a calming night wash but not enough to rank it highest; moderate interest in emotional benefits.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, did you find most appealing about the Dove Night Wash concept? A: offers a calming, stress-reducing shower experience with ingredients like chamomile and lavender clinically shown to reduce stress hormones
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product that promotes stress relief, they might consider it as a secondary option for occasional use but not as a primary choice for daily cleansing.

nodes.label: partner-scent-bonding-low-priority
nodes.description: Ranks Yours & Mine last, indicating that the social bonding aspect via shared fragrance is not a strong driver.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: Now that you've seen all 5 concepts, please rank them from most to least appealing. Pick your top 3. A: ["Dove Skip", "Dove 60-Second Body Spray", "Dove Night Wash", "Dove Skin ID", "Dove Yours & Mine"]
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product that emphasizes couple bonding or shared fragrance, they would be less likely to prioritize it and more likely to focus on individual functional benefits.

nodes.label: quick-routine-integrator
nodes.description: Interested in 60-second body spray for busy days to fit into a quick routine, valuing speed without sacrificing effectiveness.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, did you find most appealing about the Dove 60-Second Body Spray concept? A: fits my need for quick, effective cleansing on busy days
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product that promises speed and convenience for busy schedules, they would be attracted and likely to consider it for days when time is limited.

nodes.label: separate-step-eliminator
nodes.description: Appreciates products that eliminate separate steps in the routine, like skipping lotion, to save time and effort.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, did you find most appealing about the Dove Skip concept? A: eliminating the need for a separate lotion, which saves time and effort
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product that consolidates steps (e.g., 2-in-1), they would see it as a time-saver and value-add, increasing purchase likelihood.

nodes.label: evidence-driven-believer
nodes.description: Believability of claims is important; often rates claims as 'somewhat believable' but wants more evidence to fully trust.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: How believable are the claims made about the Dove Skip? A: Somewhat believable
[SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove Skip concept? A: I would want more clinical data or dermatologist endorsements
nodes.predictive_value: When claims are backed by solid evidence, they are more likely to believe and act on them; otherwise they remain skeptical.

nodes.label: price-to-size-evaluator
nodes.description: Wants larger size for better value at a given price point, assessing quantity relative to cost.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove 60-Second Body Spray concept? A: possibly a slightly larger size for better value at the price point
nodes.predictive_value: When comparing products, they would consider the size/quantity relative to price to assess value, and may choose the option with better volume per rupee.

nodes.label: skin-type-consideration
nodes.description: Wants assurance that a product works for their specific skin type before trying, to avoid irritation or inefficacy.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove Skip concept? A: assurance it works for my skin type before paying the premium price
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product that is marketed for specific skin types or offers customization, they would want to know if it matches their skin type before considering purchase.

nodes.label: ingredient-transparency-seeker
nodes.description: Wants ingredient transparency to understand what is in the product and assess its suitability.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove Skin ID concept? A: ingredient transparency to ensure the AI customization truly works
nodes.predictive_value: When provided with detailed ingredient information and explanations, they would be more trusting and likely to consider the product.

nodes.label: subscription-aversion
nodes.description: Prefers not to have a subscription model for personalized products, wanting a one-time purchase option to reduce risk.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: What, if anything, would you change or improve about the Dove Skin ID concept? A: without subscription to reduce risk
nodes.predictive_value: When offered a subscription-based purchase model, they would likely prefer a one-time purchase option or may avoid the product altogether.

nodes.label: brand-fit-evaluator
nodes.description: Considers how well a new concept fits with the brand's image (Dove), rating fit as 'fits somewhat well' for all concepts.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Q: How well does the Dove 60-Second Body Spray idea fit with what you expect from the Dove brand? A: Fits somewhat well
[SURVEY] Q: How well does the Dove Skip idea fit with what you expect from the Dove brand? A: Fits somewhat well
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a new product from a known brand, they will assess whether it aligns with their perception of that brand; a poor fit may reduce credibility.
